Survey_Instrument.QuestionText: Please consider whether there have been significant times during the last 12 months during which the following statements applied to you. Then please select the option that best describes how well each statement described you during that period. 1. Not at all 2. A little 3. Moderately 4. A lot
My mind wandered far away from the present.
I stole from someone I knew without their knowledge.
I was rigid and inflexible in my approach to doing things.
I collected things I did not need.
I enjoyed rebelling against authority figures (e.g., police, teachers, bosses).
I could have improved on my organizational skills.
I was proud of myself.
I was afraid of heights.
I had a hard time staying in my seat when I was supposed to be seated.
I counted things over and over again.
I didn't express my emotions.
I experienced loss of consciousness or had fainting spells (in the absence of a diagnosed medical disorder)
I felt like I could keep going and going without ever getting tired.
I spread rumors about other people.
I could control things with my mind.
I got so angry that I yelled or screamed.
I enjoyed thinking about my future and the great things it would bring.
If I passed by a reflective surface (like a window), I checked how I looked.
I found sex physically uncomfortable.
I was afraid that I might suffer from a serious illness
I felt compelled to count things.
People told me that I was too perfectionistic.
I got angry about small things.
I had periods of unexplained deafness.
I found romantic relationships to be tiresome.
Life felt too short to follow other peoples' rules.
I avoided small public spaces (such as an office or restaurant).
I had the experience that everything suddenly seemed unfamiliar and I forgot where I was.
I had a recurring bad dream.
I was not all that concerned with other peoples' needs.
I turned down social invitations so that I could spend more time gaming.
I was sensitive to changes in my body.
I examined every single option before making a selection.
I felt that the world is a dangerous place.
I experienced chest pain.
I used certain medications (e.g. laxatives, diuretics) to lose weight.
Most people would have liked to be like me.
I felt sexually inadequate.
I found sexual activity with someone unconscious (e.g., asleep or passed out from alcohol or a drug) sexually arousing.
It was difficult for me to touch something that was dirty.
I recognized that I had a lot of special qualities.
I got anxious when others were watching me.
I engaged in risky sexual activity.
I felt shy around other people.
I didn't care whether my behavior was considered proper or not.
I worried a lot about my health.
I strove for perfection, even when others said that I should move on.
I was acutely aware of what was going on with my body.
I reached orgasm later than I wanted to.
It was very important for me to take the lead.
When in a group, I liked to be in control.
People talked behind my back.
I had no interest in romantic relationships.
I had trouble planning and keeping to schedules.
I looked for ways to make time for gaming.
I was happiest when I was alone.
I preferred to do things the safest way possible.
I felt down and discouraged.
I felt the need to stand up when I should have been sitting down.
I didn't get excited about very much.
I couldn�t stop comparing my body to those of others.
It was painful for me to have (or try to have) penetrative vaginal or anal intercourse.
I found it enjoyable to hurt animals.
I was easily distracted.
I felt that I did not want to be in a close relationship.
I avoided performing or giving a talk in front of others.
I became very anxious during a storm.
Most people were just out for themselves.
People told me that I was being inappropriate.
I was afraid that I might be having a heart attack.
I was trembling or shaking.
I had little or no interest in sexual activity.
I felt worthless.
Seeing something gory made me woozy.
I did not really care how others felt.
People told me that I work too much.
I got angry when people did not take my health problems seriously.
I quit working (my job, homework, or housework) before I was finished.
I was so focused on being productive that I had less time for friends and family.
I was disgusted with myself.
I ate a very large amount of food in a short period of time (e.g., within 2 hours).
I found it difficult to reach orgasm.
I expected to get treated better than others.
My mind felt mixed up.
I was unable to control when I reached orgasm.
I hid my emotions.
I was grouchy.
I noticed small changes to how my body feels.
Even when I was very careful, I worried whether I had done something correctly.
I had trouble remembering things.
I exercised when I was injured.
Romantic relationships seemed like a hassle to me.
I liked being the person who has the authority.
I felt optimistic.
I felt cheerful.
I could feel changes in my body.
I demanded perfection from myself.
I felt motivated.
I kept gambling even though it caused problems in my relationships.
Some people thought I was self-centered.
I had to cancel plans in order to get my exercise in.
I was a messy person.
I spent time planning how to reduce my food intake.
Major decisions took months of careful consideration to arrive at a choice.
I was something of a workaholic.
I had trouble staying asleep.
I blamed myself for things.
I was overwhelmed by anxiety.
I felt dizzy or lightheaded.
I picked at areas of my skin that I don�t like.
I was so worked up I thought I might die.
Being muscular was very important to me.
I found it easy to deceive others.
I had a hard time asserting myself to others.
I carefully weighed out the pros and cons before I made any decision.
I used medicines or supplements to bulk up.
I spent a lot of time getting ready before going out.
I felt an urge to pull my hair.
I missed appointments because I forgot about them.
I tried to break up someone else's friendship or romantic relationship.
My fantasies felt very real to me.
I found watching unsuspecting people who were naked, masturbating, or having sex sexually arousing.
I thought that certain numbers were lucky for me.
People told me I exercised too much.
I failed to consider the consequences of an action.
My mind was flooded with troubling images of a bad experience.
Minor frustrations really upset me.
I felt that things around me were not real.
I was dissatisfied with the shape of my body.
I was embarrassed because I pulled my hair.
I continued to do things the same way, even though my approach clearly wasn�t working.
I could not resist eating.
I was prone to daydreaming.
I struggled to think clearly.
I felt that other persons were acting against me.
The sight of a needle upset me.
I exercised more than was good for me.
I preferred to stay home than to go to a party.
I tried to eat only healthy foods.
I pulled out hairs that were a different color or texture.
I could not resist buying something that I did not need.
Nothing seemed interesting to me.
I took time to consider all possible outcomes before making any decision.
I spent too much time thinking about illnesses I might have.
I did not concern myself with helping people.
I was unable to reach orgasm.
I vomited to control my weight.
I was the dominant person in my relationships.
I lost myself in a fictional character.
I felt very stressed.
I considered using syrup of ipecac, enemas, or suppositories to lose weight.
I bit myself on purpose.
My imagination held my attention for hours.
I was willing to tell a small lie to make things easier for myself.
I felt uninterested during sexual activity
I felt like I was outside of my body.
I suddenly felt as if a traumatic event were actually happening again.
I believed other people could actually hear my thoughts.
I did something spontaneous that I later regretted.
I did not have much sympathy for others.
I did not like my weight (the number on the scale).
I didn�t pay much attention to social standards of behavior.
I was easily startled.
I felt a lot more hyper or active than usual.
I got in trouble with the law.
I had pains in several parts of my body.
I tensed up during sexual activity due to pain.
I was very cautious about taking risks.
I felt the need to wash my hands again and again.
I felt restless when I had to sit still for more than ten minutes.
I thought a lot about death.
I could not feel things I touched.
I had difficulty making eye contact with others.
During sex, I usually climaxed very quickly.
I worked on a plan to kill myself.
I could not return to a task after getting distracted.
I thought it may be possible for other people to read my mind, or for me to read other's minds.
The sight of an insect (e.g., a cockroach) upset me.
I cut myself on purpose.
I was troubled by unexplained seizures or convulsions.
Special messages were written in papers or announced on TV just for me.
I liked having power.
I kept my shopping a secret.
I hardly showed any feelings.
I smelled things that were not actually there.
I deserved special treatment.
I used sex to relieve negative feelings (e.g., anxiety, sadness, anger).
I woke up early and could not get back to sleep.
People would describe me as a risk-taker.
My temper scared people.
I felt like I would be unable to breathe in a small, tight, or enclosed space.
I found touching or rubbing against a stranger sexually arousing.
I did not feel like spending time with people.
At times, I just wanted to feel pain.
I enjoyed being the center of attention.
I found it easy to manipulate others.
I forced someone to have sex with me.
I engaged in sexual activities that I later regretted.
I experienced strange sights or sounds.
People admired me.
My thoughts kept jumping from one thing to another.
I wished I could change certain parts of my body.
My mood was negatively affected when I was unable to game as long as I wanted.
I was very irritable.
My skin picking created problems for me.
I was frustrated with having to convince others I had a real illness.
I put a lot of thought into ideas before I took any action.
I did not feel much like eating.
I was very set in my ways.
I felt faint at the sight of blood.
I spent more money shopping than I could afford.
I lost a significant amount of weight without even trying.
I was afraid of flying.
I had trouble telling whether something really happened or I just imagined it.
I had an illness or disease that others dismissed.
I used substances during sex to encourage my partner to use substances too.
After I left my home, I worried that I failed to do something.
I refused to compromise because I knew I was right.
I felt like I was in danger.
It was difficult for me to stay sexually aroused once sexual activity had started.
I avoided taking risks at all costs.
I hurt myself on purpose.
I had trouble standing up for myself.
I broke into houses, buildings, or cars.
I was ashamed of myself.
I wished I lived in my fantasies instead of real life.
I avoided contact with people because I was worried about germs.
I refused to budge as a matter of principle, even on small matters.
My work success was the most valuable aspect of my life.
I slept very poorly.
It was important to me to build muscle mass and eliminate body fat.
I was fearful of rats or mice.
I used substances to reduce anxiety during sexual activity.
I was uncomfortable meeting new people.
I avoided foods because I did not like their appearance
I was afraid that I would have a panic attack in a small, tight, or enclosed space.
I was afraid of getting stung or bitten.
I said things that were not true.
I didn't experience the joy and pleasure that most other people do.
Some people thought I was uncaring and unfeeling.
I had a dream so distressing that it continued to affect me after waking.
I lost things that I needed.
When in public, I liked to stand out.
I was very confident in myself around other people.
My mood changed suddenly for no good reason.
I was afraid of getting trapped in a crowd.
I felt an urge to hurt myself (cut, burn, bite, etc.).
My needs and wants came first.
I felt like I could go for days without sleeping.
I had a nagging doubt that I'd failed to do something important.
I quickly went from being sad to being happy.
I acted on impulse.
I was a thrill-seeker.
I avoided numbers that could bring bad luck.
I allowed people to take advantage of me.
During sex, I consciously tried to delay orgasm so I could last longer.
I was unhappy about my sexual performance.
It was hard to stop gambling once I started.
I paid my bills late or missed other important deadlines.
I believed it was fine to tell a little lie in order to get out of trouble.
My heart was racing or pounding.
I had much more energy than usual.
I felt that my work must be flawless.
I did things to get others to notice me.
I went out of my way to avoid risks.
I experienced a sudden sensory loss (e.g., seeing or hearing problems) that doctors could not explain.
Reading articles about disease made me worry about my health.
I engaged in unprotected sex.
I wanted well-defined stomach muscles.
I was bothered by several bodily symptoms (e.g., headache, fatigue or stomach problems) for which there was no clear or sufficient medical explanation.
I felt like I no longer existed.
I felt worn out.
I did things on purpose that were against the rules.
I went a long time without eating.
I said things without thinking.
I saw a person but then realized it was just a shadow.
I felt hopeless about the future.
I dressed so as to call attention to myself.
People said I am strange.
I vandalized or defaced public property.
I bought much more than I needed.
I worried about getting germs through contact with animals.
I avoided foods because of their smell or texture.
I tried to limit the number of fatty foods I ate.
I avoided small, tight, or enclosed spaces.
My sexual fantasies strained my relationships.
I had trouble sitting still.
I lied about myself to other people.
Others could not be trusted.
I bet more money than I could really afford to lose.
I was very suspicious of other people.
Many types of food disgusted me.
I focused on eating more healthy foods.
I avoided people who might bring back bad memories.
My desire for things to be flawless kept me from getting everything done.
I tried to eat fewer snacks.
I found myself worrying whether I actually did something or only intended to do it.
I had periods of unexplained blindness.
I felt little desire for sexual activity
I kept gaming, even though it caused problems at work or school.
I had blank spells or periods of missing time.
I was unable to concentrate on a task when it was uninteresting to me.
I kept items that I did not need.
I enjoyed activities that were a little bit dangerous.
I tried to conceal areas where I had picked at my skin.
I made people believe almost anything.
My compulsive buying caused me financial problems.
I thought I was the only one chosen by God for a special role.
I was happiest when I was in a romantic relationship.
I became disinterested in tasks easily.
I said unusual things.
I avoided areas where there might be snakes or lizards.
I felt something was wrong with my body.
I tried to kill myself.
I felt guilty about sexual difficulties
Interacting with others was not very important to me.
I had a sensation that something was crawling under my skin.
I liked attracting the attention of others.
It was difficult to find healthcare providers who recognized my illness.
I did not like taking chances.
I found it difficult to throw things away.
Took a lot of effort to get going.
I felt good about myself.
I threatened someone with a weapon like a bat, brick, knife, or gun.
I spent a lot of time grooming myself.
I skipped two meals in a row.
Bulking up muscle while staying lean was a main fitness goal for me.
I had trouble falling asleep.
Others noticed that I was unusually excited or up.
People told me I was coldhearted.
My muscles were weak.
I paid close attention to how my body feels.
I was concerned that other persons might bring me harm.
I was jealous of what other people have.
I used substances to make my partner more appealing.
I was easily annoyed.
I mocked people.
I had to clean myself because I felt contaminated.
I spent a lot of time looking at myself.
I found it hard to resist the urge to shop.
Things went best when I told others what to do.
I avoided activities where I would need to sit down for long.
I insulted others.
My home was so cluttered that it was difficult to get around.
I was obsessed with cleanliness.
I felt the urge to check to make sure I really had done something.
I thought about killing myself.
I was cautious.
I was worried about my sex life.
I needed to count to a certain number before starting an activity.
Once I started eating, I could not stop.
I was afraid of spiders.
I saw things that were not really there.
Nightmares caused me to wake up at night.
I did not experience enough sexual arousal (penile erection or vaginal lubrication) to enjoy sex.
I was afraid of the dark.
Others could easily take advantage of me because I was too passive.
I paid a lot of attention to how I look.
I made decisions only after I had all the information I could get.
I made excuses to avoid eating.
I forged checks or used other people�s credit cards without permission.
I avoided places that reminded me of something bad that happened.
Sexual activity was even more enjoyable for me when I was intoxicated/high.
I was never on time.
I intentionally scratched my skin so severely that it started bleeding.
I had so much energy that others couldn�t keep up with me.
I heard things that no one else could hear.
I was a rule breaker.
It was difficult for me to get sexually aroused.
People were basically selfish with little concern for others.
My moods were intense and unpredictable.
Even on days off, I found myself thinking about work.
People told me that I was a very picky eater.
I didn't respond to things that other people found emotional.
I exercised until I felt sick.
People said I could be a bully.
I thought about things in unusual ways.
I felt depressed.
I was much more talkative than usual.
I found exposing my genitals to a stranger sexually arousing.
My appetite was poor.
I enjoyed looking in the mirror.
I had repeated memories of a traumatic event.
People told me that I do not show emotions.
Some people were out to harm me.
I had angry outbursts.
I tried to con or cheat other people.
People told me that I did not eat enough.
I was bothered by stomach symptoms (diarrhea, nausea, stomach pain etc.)
I made decisions quickly without thinking them through.
I felt on guard and on edge.
I was watchful for danger.
I taunted others.
I felt nervous and on edge.
I was selfish.
I had very little energy.
I was unable to resist the impulse to gamble.
When I had the chance, I chose to be alone rather than with other people.
I believed that if you are not careful, other people will take advantage of you.
I experienced sudden muscle weakness or paralysis.
I reached orgasm sooner than I wanted to.
I quit tasks that became too challenging.
Taking risks made me feel alive.
Gambling caused me to go into debt.
Many people thought that my behavior was really strange.
